name: Cova des Pinar
access: Own vehicle, or EMT bus line 8 (Puerta de San Antonio - Son Roca)
equipment: 15 m rope, 1 sling, 1 carabiner, 1 rope protector
description: A moderately-sized cave on the summit of Puig Rodó near La Vileta, richly decorated with formations throughout giving a baroque appearance. Features gours, stalagmitic masses, columns, and crawlways reaching a maximum depth of -14 m. Considered of both speleological and archaeological interest.
full_description:
Introduction

This is a cavity of not too large dimensions, situated on the summit of Puig Rodó, near one of the paths that connects La Vileta with the Coves del Pilar and the San Pancracio mines.

Description

Once inside, we will immediately realize that we are in a cavity fully covered with formations, as they surround us everywhere, giving a sense of baroque extravagance in cave form. We can begin the route through the western end, being careful as the floor in this area is quite slippery. As we advance over a floor carpeted with stones, we pass different stalagmitic masses on our right. After passing a couple of gours, we will find the small entrance that gives access to a small room that can be entered without progression equipment. It is not a place of great interest, but it is part of the cavity. The deepest part of the cave in this area is a little further below at -8 m. We can continue the visit heading NE, first passing over a small gour and then a larger one. On the left and on a ledge, formations pile up, occupying a large part of the central zone of the cave. Once past the gour, we can continue our route either by skirting the aforementioned ledge or through a crawlway of approximately half a dozen meters that runs beneath it. Whether we take one option or the other, the destination is the same: the interior of a small pit from which we will tackle the final section. To do so, we will have to overcome a low-ceiling area that will require us to put our crawling skills into practice until we reach -14 m, the maximum depth of the cave. We will return the same way until reaching the large gour area, from where we can venture into the central zone of the cave where the greatest number of formations are found, including notable stalagmitic masses, columns, gours, etc.

The Cova des Pinar can be considered of speleological interest, as well as archaeological.

As it is located in a prolific area for caves, we can head to any other one to complete the day.
